cleaned_readable_excerpt_x: OF THE ANCIENT LUNAR ASTERISMS. BY T. W. KINGSMILL. Astronomical history may be said to have had its beginning about the year 2150 B.C. when the astronomers of Chaldea substituted solar for lunar observations and marked out the zodiac into the twelve constellations which with little change still hold sway. Prior to the introduction of the reform the moon had kept guard over the calendar, and the commencement of the year was connected with the position of the full moon in the zodiacal asterisms.

Prior to the introduction of the reform the moon had kept guard over the calendar, and the commencement of the year was connected with the position of the full moon in the zodiacal asterisms.

title: A Comparative table of the ancient lunar asterisms
